episodes.title: From Cult to Cam Girl: Trauma, Addiction & Healing | Ex-Jehovah's Witness Women | Roxanne's Story
episodes.description:
In this powerful episode, Roxanne @theroxannemiller  shares her raw and deeply personal story of growing up in a high-control religious environment and the lasting impact it had on her life.

Born into the Jehovah’s Witnesses, Roxanne opens up about childhood trauma, abuse she endured, and the pressure to suppress her identity from a young age. After leaving the organization, she found herself navigating a world she was never prepared for, turning to the adult entertainment industry, alcohol, tattoos and food as a way to cope and numb the pain.

She vulnerably shares how being raised to seek external validation shaped her choices, how shunning from her family affected her sense of self, and the difficult journey of recognizing and healing from deep-rooted trauma.
This is not just a story about leaving Jehovah’s Witnesses, it's about survival, self-discovery, and the long road to healing.
_________________________________________

My mission is to create a safe space where women feel empowered to use their voices and share their stories because I truly believe your experiences can inspire and help others.

With love,
Alissa
#exjw  #exjehovahswitness  #cultsurvivor  #cults  #leavingjehovahswitnesses

episodes.title: How I Found Out My Grandma Is Dying..... | The Reality of Jehovah's Witness Shunning
Lifelong Consequences After a Short Time as a JW | Ex-Jehovah's Witness Women | Beata's Story
episodes.description:
Today I had the privilege of sitting down with Beata as she shares her powerful journey; from seeking out the Jehovah’s Witnesses as a teenager in Poland, to being forced to flee to Australia due to religious persecution. As a refuge in Australia, Beata saw a different side of the organization that helped her to start to truly see this wasn't the "truth". 

Beata discusses her upbringing, her spiritual awakening, and the challenges she faced within the religion. From seeing first hand how predators are handled, to witnessing the conditional love and support. Beata used everything she experienced as a way to analyze this organization she was told was the truth. And now she courageously shares her experience of leaving, overcoming abuse, and reclaiming her voice.

Her story is one of resilience, critical thinking, and rediscovering self-worth, showing what healing and true independence can look like after leaving a high-control environment.
_________________________________________

My mission is to create a safe space where women feel empowered to use their voices and share their stories because I truly believe your experiences can inspire and help others.

With love,
Alissa

#exjw #exjehovahswitness #cultsurvivor #cults #leavingjehovahswitnesses

episodes.title: "I Was Falsely Accused of Apostasy and Lost Everything" | Deanna's Story
episodes.description:
Today I had the privilege of sitting down with Deanna for ‪@CultToFreedom‬     she shares of how she lost everything but has since completely rebuilt her life. 

Raised as a fifth-generation Jehovah’s Witness, Deanna was deeply involved in the organization from a young age. From helping build the Mexico Bethel facility, to being a need greater in Dominican Republic, she was heavily involved and fully believed. Everything changed when she was falsely labeled an apostate. She opens up about the devastating consequences that followed, including losing her reputation, her support system, and even custody of her children during a custody battle where religion became a major issue. 

Despite remaining faithful at the time, she was treated as an enemy and pushed out of the very community she had dedicated her life to.

Deanna also shares how she eventually rebuilt her life after leaving Jehovah’s Witnesses. From losing everything in her late 30s to creating a completely new life, her story is one of resilience, healing, and rediscovering identity outside of a high-control religion.

Contact Deanna: healingsoulsnlp@gmail.com

episodes.title: "I Had to Shed My Victim Story" | Sherry’s Story
episodes.description:
Today, I had the honor of sitting down with Sherry to hear her powerful story. From a traumatic childhood to joining the Jehovah’s Witnesses at just 14 years old. She endured unimaginable trauma and witnessed firsthand how abuse victims were handled within the organization.

But her story doesn’t end there.

After walking away, Sherry made the brave decision to stop living from a place of victimhood and start reclaiming her life. What followed has been a journey of healing, growth, and incredible accomplishments. Today, she lives with purpose, freedom, and genuine joy.

In this conversation, Sherry shares hope for anyone who has experienced trauma: your story is not over. What happened to you does not define you. Healing is possible and victory is still within reach.

Sherry’s website: http://www.sweetfreedomlife.com/

episodes.title: Jehovah’s Witness Blood Doctrine Change LEAKED | Are They Admitting They Were Wrong?
episodes.description: New light was leaked early and as of Friday March 20 it was confirmed via a Governing Body update. This one got me fired up! I sincerely hope more people continue to open their eyes and wake up in the cult that is, Jehovah's Witnesses.

episodes.title: I Was Indoctrinated Behind My Parents Back | Ex-Jehovah's Witness Women | Carolina's Story
episodes.description:
Today I had the privilege of sitting down with Carolina as she shares her story of how she was indoctrinated from a baby without her own parents ever being Jehovah's Witnesses. Though she was never baptized she discusses the manipulation, control, and indoctrination she experienced from a young age, the long-term effects on her mental health, and the importance of setting boundaries. She wanted to share her story to warn parents about the dangers of allowing your children around Jehovah's Witnesses, even when you believe you have clear boundaries in place. 

_________________________________________

My mission is to create a safe space where women feel empowered to use their voices and share their stories because I truly believe your experiences can inspire and help others.

With love,

episodes.title: Why You Should Say NO to the Jehovah’s Witness Memorial
episodes.description:
Every year around this time, thousands of former Jehovah’s Witnesses receive the same message: “Are you coming to the Memorial this year?”

For many ex-JWs, that invitation isn’t just a simple question, it’s often filled with pressure, guilt, and emotional expectations from family still inside the organization. 

In this video, I talk about why saying “no” to the Jehovah’s Witness Memorial can be one of the most important boundaries you set after leaving.
Many people attend the Memorial just to keep the peace with family. But what message does that actually send? And why does attending often keep the cycle of pressure going year after year?
_________________________________________

My mission is to create a safe space where people feel empowered to use their voices and share their stories because I truly believe your experiences can inspire and help others.

With love,
Alissa

episodes.title: "I Was Forced to Live a Lie" | Sandra's Story
episodes.description: During this conversation, I sat down with Sandra to share her raw and honest journey. She wasn’t born into the religion, she joined in her twenties, after being raised Catholic. Over the next 20 years, she endured deeply painful and challenging experiences. Her story sheds light not only on the misogyny that is rampant within the organization, but also on how easily a woman can become trapped in an unhealthy marriage.

episodes.title: "I Asked to Be Disfellowshipped" | Phey's Story
episodes.description: In this conversation Phey discusses life growing up Jehovah's Witness, how she had doubts creep in around 15 and what her life looked like after she asked to be disfellowshipped to finally break free. She talks a lot about deconstructing and rebuilding her life, and how others can do the same.

episodes.title: "My Life as a Jehovah's Witness Was Full of Pain" | Dominique's Story
episodes.description: This conversation talks about how vulnerable people can be taken advantage of, the pressure and isolation that often happens inside the community, and the doubts and expectations that shaped Dominique's life for years. We also talk about the impact of trauma and depression, how assault was handled inappropriately, and what it’s like to go through loss with very little real support. We talk about how love and acceptance in the organization is conditional, what life looks like after leaving, and some advice for people who are questioning or thinking about getting out.

episodes.title: "I Endured Abuse but Found My Voice!" | Brooke's Story
episodes.description: This conversation dives into Brooke's story; from childhood trauma, unhealthy family dynamics, and the impact of living as a Jehovah's Witness. It explores the experience of shame, rejection, and the consequences of teenage rebellion. We also discuss disfellowshipping, elders' abuse, control, and the silent suffering and stigma associated with seeking help or leaving an abusive relationship within the Jehovah's Witness community.

Today’s voicemail is from a woman who has been shunned for over 15 years. She shares the emotional turmoil recent changes to the shunning policy have caused in her life and the difficult position she now faces. Should she attend her sister’s wedding through Zoom, knowing that’s the only way she’s allowed? Or should she protect her peace and decline?
If you’ve been in a similar situation, share your advice and experience in the comments below.

episodes.title: "I Was Baptized at 7! But FREE by 23!" | Sam's Story
episodes.description: The conversation delves into Sam's childhood experiences within the Jehovah's Witnesses and the impact of family dynamics on her mental health. It explores the pressure to conform, the struggle for acceptance, and the emotional toll of abusive relationships within the organization. The conversation delves into her surviving trauma within the Jehovah's Witness organization, reporting the assault, and facing reproach and isolation. It then explores Sam's journey of leaving the organization, healing, and speaking out to empower others who have faced similar challenges.

episodes.title: I Refused to Raise My Kids in This Cult | Ex-Jehovah's Witness Women | Audrey's Story
episodes.description:
Find the Interview Contact Form, Ask Alissa, and all other links here:👇🏼
https://linktr.ee/alissasawake
______________________________________

Today I had the privilege of sitting down with Audrey as she shares her powerful and deeply personal story about growing up as one of Jehovah’s Witnesses and the journey that eventually led her to question the organization she was raised to trust.

Audrey’s family was first introduced to the religion after a simple knock on the door from Jehovah’s Witnesses, a moment that would ultimately shape the course of her childhood and family life. In this conversation, she reflects on what it was like growing up inside a high-control religious environment, including the loss of birthdays and holidays, the expectations placed on young members, and the constant pressure to conform.

Audrey also shares the heartbreaking loss of her father as a result of the organization’s blood transfusion policy, a tragedy that profoundly impacted her life and became a pivotal moment in her journey. Facing that loss forced her to begin asking difficult questions about doctrine, authority, and the cost of unquestioned loyalty.

Through honesty and reflection, Audrey discusses the gradual process of questioning, researching, and ultimately finding the courage to think critically about the beliefs she once accepted without question.

Her story sheds light on the emotional and personal realities many experience when leaving Jehovah’s Witnesses, and it serves as an important reminder that questioning and seeking truth are powerful steps toward healing and freedom.

If you’ve ever struggled with faith, religious control, or rebuilding your life after leaving a high-control religion, Audrey’s story is one you won’t want to miss.
______________________________________

My mission is to create a safe space where women feel empowered to use their voices and share their stories because I truly believe your experiences can inspire and help others.

With love,
Alissa

episodes.title: "I Broke the Cycle: 3 Generations Saved from the Jehovah's Witnesses" | Patty's Story
episodes.description: The conversation delves into the impact of the 1975 Armageddon prediction on Patty's life, as well as the challenges of growing up as a Jehovah's Witness. It explores the struggles of childhood and adolescence, family dynamics, marriage, rebellion, and the process of fading and leaving the organization. Patty's journey reflects the disillusionment and doubts that arose from the organization's teachings and the impact of the 1975 prediction on her faith. The conversation delves into the devastating impact of shunning, the challenges of life after disfellowship, the reality of shunning, finding faith and healing, the impact on children and grandchildren, emotional hooks and healing, and life outside the organization. Patty also shares her journey of healing and recovery, offering advice and hope to those in similar situations.

episodes.title: "I Was Too Independent for the Misogynistic Jehovah's Witness Culture" | Summer's Story
episodes.description: The conversation delves into the mental health impact of shunning and the challenges faced by Jehovah's Witness women. It explores the childhood and family dynamics, struggles as a teenage Jehovah's Witness, experiences of marriage, divorce, and disfellowshipping, the treatment by elders, the impact of misogyny, and the journey towards independence and leaving the organization. The conversation covers the experience of life inside the Jehovah's Witnesses organization, the process of awakening and leaving, the emotional impact of shunning, the journey of building a new life, and the importance of finding community and pursuing personal passions post-organization.

episodes.title: "I Was the Ideal Jehovah's Witness"  Former Pioneer & Elder's Wife Speaks Out | Lori's Story
episodes.description: The conversation covers the journey of Lori, who grew up in the Jehovah's Witness community, her experiences, and the impact of leaving the organization. It delves into her childhood, adolescence, marriage, pioneer & elder's wife experience, and the awakening that led to her departure from the organization. The conversation delves into the experiences of leaving a religious cult, highlighting the challenges faced by individuals and families. It explores the impact of religious trauma, cultural and social pressures, misogyny, and gender discrimination within the organization. The discussion also touches on the dynamics of Bethel and the process of leaving the organization to find freedom and healing.

episodes.title: I Found Jesus After Leaving the Jehovah’s Witnesses | Ex-Jehovah's Witness Women | Ardys's Story
episodes.description:
If you have a story to tell, reach out to me: 
https://forms.gle/bLzaA1c3Hqw7ikQ26

If you have a question, leave me a message:
https://www.askalissa.com
_________________________________________

Today, I had the honor of sitting down with Ardys to share her powerful and deeply personal story.

Introduced to the organization at a young age, Ardys always had a sincere love for God. As a teenager, she faced an unplanned pregnancy that led to a marriage she wasn’t ready for, setting the stage for years of emotional and spiritual struggles.

For a long time, she found herself going in and out of the organization, wrestling with the need for family validation and the belief that her life was falling apart because she had stepped away. But everything changed when she discovered a genuine, personal relationship with Jesus, one that didn’t depend on performance, approval, or religious expectations.

Ardys’s journey is a powerful reminder that doubt doesn’t disqualify you. Healing is possible, faith can be rebuilt, and a real relationship with God can make you whole.

Keep an eye out for her upcoming ebook and podcast! 
✨Ebook: After the Watchtower & Awakened to the Real Word of God: A Practical Guide for Ex–Jehovah’s Witnesses Rebuilding Faith, Identity, Healing, and Life in Christ
✨Podcast: Awakened and Anchored with ARDYS

episodes.title: Elders Told Me I Asked Too Many Questions | Ex-Jehovah's Witness Women | Eva’s Story
episodes.description:
If you have a story to tell, reach out to me: 
https://forms.gle/bLzaA1c3Hqw7ikQ26

If you have a question, leave me a message:
https://www.askalissa.com
_________________________________________

Today I spoke with Eva, who has been out for 22 years. She shared how her 16-year-old son’s disfellowshipping became the turning point that pushed her to seriously question everything. When an elder told her she was asking too many questions, she walked out of the Kingdom Hall and never returned.

She explained that her doubts began as early as 8 years old. As a teenager, she watched 1975 come and go, and often found herself on the fringes of the congregation, making choices that left her carrying a heavy sense of guilt.

Eva's story shows the pain that comes when you leave, when your entire family shuns you but she it also shows the beauty that comes with surrounding yourself with those who truly love you, unconditionally. 
_________________________________________

My mission is to create a safe space where women feel empowered to use their voices and share their stories because I truly believe your experiences can inspire and help others.

Thank you so much for the support, and I’ll see you in the next video!

With love,
Alissa

Everything changed when a close friend confided in her about being a victim of SA. She believed she was doing the right thing by reporting it to the congregation, but instead of support, she was silenced and treated like an outcast. Her story reveals how abuse allegations are handled within the organization and the painful reality of losing your entire community through shunning.

episodes.title: DEAR ALISSA: Ex-Jehovah's Witness Anonymous Letter | COVID & Our Teens Mistreatment Woke Us Up
episodes.description:
Today I share our first anonymous letter. Born and raised in the organization, this woman and her family began to question everything after elders mistreated their 15 year old pregnant daughter. From mischaracterizing her repentance to publicly singling her out, they tried to hold on by telling themselves they were serving Jehovah, not men. But when COVID arrived, those questions intensified as the level of control and overreach from the Governing Body became impossible to ignore.

To the woman who trusted me with her story, thank you for your bravery and vulnerability. Please show her and her family your love and support in the comments.

episodes.title: ASK ALISSA Is Live: Anonymous Voicemails for Life Inside or Outside the Jehovah’s Witnesses
episodes.description:
https://www.askalissa.com

Ask Alissa is a safe, anonymous, and judgment free space to ask real questions about life as a Jehovah’s Witness. This includes doubts you may be afraid to voice, questions about leaving, or how to rebuild your life after.

**Disclaimer:** I’m not a licensed professional. What I share is based on lived experience and personal understanding, not therapy or clinical advice.

**Privacy Notice:** Voicemails may be shared on my YouTube channel. If you wish to remain anonymous, please avoid using your real name or sharing identifying details such as your location.

This year, my goal has been to support this community in deeper and more meaningful ways. I believe this voicemail system is another step toward that commitment. I will offer guidance the same way I would to a friend, honestly, thoughtfully, and with care.

Thank you for the continued support you show me and the compassion you extend to those who bravely share their stories here. Let’s keep building one another up.

With love,
Alissa

episodes.title: I Spent Years Pretending While Planning My Escape | Ex-Jehovah's Witness Woman | Paulina’s Story
episodes.description:
Welcome back for another Ex-Jehovah's Witness Women Speak Out episode where I sit down for real and honest conversations.

This week, I sat down with Paulina who spent years playing the role of the perfect Jehovah's Witness, all while try to plan her escape. Her story sheds light on not only how hard it truly is to leave, but on how outward appearances don't always show what the person is internally going through. 

Paulina's Social Media:
https://www.instagram.com/paupauponc3/
https://www.tiktok.com/@paupauponc3
Email: jakepauponce2025@gmail.com

I look forward to sharing more powerful stories from former #jehovahswitness women, each with their own unique journey, yet connected by shared experiences. 

If you want to tell your story, reach out to me via this form:
https://forms.gle/bt4LYmYPCJqdYuS37

My mission is to create a safe space where women feel empowered to use their voices and share their stories because I truly believe your experiences can inspire and help others.

Thank you so much for the support, and I’ll see you in the next video!

Alissa

#ExJehovahsWitness
#ExJW
#ExJWWomen
#LeavingJehovahsWitnesses
#ExJWStories
#CultRecovery

episodes.title: Kristy Sarah & Desmond Scott Divorce EXPOSES Jehovah’s Witness Control
episodes.description:
A high-profile influencer divorce is putting an unexpected spotlight on Jehovah’s Witnesses and their deeply controlling divorce policies. Millions are watching this breakup unfold, and wondering why divorce works so differently inside the #jehovahswitness world.  

In this video, I break down the rules:  why abuse isn’t grounds to move on, why infidelity becomes the only “escape,” and how elders and congregations police some of the most personal decisions a person can make. 

This isn’t gossip, and it’s not about attacking a couple in pain. This is about exposing a system that traps people in unhappy, unsafe marriages and calls it “God’s will.” 

If you’ve ever wondered how much control Jehovah’s Witnesses really have over their members’ lives, this conversation matters.

#exjw #exjehovahswitness #exjwsupport #cultsurvivor #jw

episodes.title: Why I Left the Jehovah's Witnesses: My Husband's Story
episodes.description:
We recorded this video back in July 2025 on our two-year disassociation anniversary. It took us some time to go through all the footage, but we’re really happy to finally share it. This is my husband’s chance to tell his story in his own words. From growing up as a Jehovah’s Witness to his personal waking-up journey, and everything in between

I want to take a moment to thank you, as always, for being such a kind and supportive community. Sharing your story can be incredibly vulnerable, but every person who speaks up helps someone else feel seen, understood, and a little less alone.  

One important disclaimer my husband asked me to share: his personal experience with disfellowshipping should never minimize how harmful and painful this practice truly is. His situation was different from many others because he was still living at home, he maintained more interaction with his family, and for him it felt more like a break than total isolation. That said, this does not invalidate or diminish the very real pain, trauma, and loss experienced by those who endured far harsher circumstances. Their feelings and experiences are completely valid.

Thank you all for being here and supporting!
-Alissa 

Alissa's Story: 
https://youtu.be/Xhn4BTtu3zI?si=cJNZHfErdw8kBgIC
Links Mentioned:

@LloydEvans

Warwick Tour: https://youtu.be/wRf8u0Ibkww?si=PBgMxBsm_GTFl4y3

https://gizmodo.com/new-whistleblower-site-faithleaks-releases-confidential-1821799936

episodes.title: Former Jehovah's Witness Decorates for Christmas | Joy, Healing and Honest Feelings
episodes.description:
Hi friends,
Today’s video is a little different from my usual content. We’re going to decorate for Christmas together while I open up about how I feel during this time of year. As a former Jehovah’s Witness who missed out on decades without any holidays, it feels incredibly special to finally create these memories with my kids and give them the childhood I never had. But I’ll be honest—alongside the joy, there’s also some sadness and heaviness as the reality of shunning still weighs on me.

So let’s decorate and unpack some feelings—holiday therapy style!

As always I appreciate all the support I've received from everyone. I truly love the community we are building here!

See you next time, with another ExJW Women Speak Out Interview! 

❤️ Alissa

episodes.title: Were We Basically in the Same Religion? Ex-JW & Ex-Mormon Compare Notes w/ @CultstoConsciousness ​
episodes.description:
I sat down with Shelise from ⁨ @CultstoConsciousness  to swap stories and compare our former faiths. When I deconstructed from the #jehovahswitness organization, I was shocked at how many striking similarities there are between Jehovah’s Witnesses and Mormons, it’s almost like they’re sister religions!

If you’re navigating your own journey out of a high-control faith, this conversation will really resonate with you.

👉 Once you’ve watched, don’t miss part two of our discussion over on the  @CultstoConsciousness  channel, where we talk about what it was like for me to see my dad again after two years. https://youtu.be/gDE-APbvYXY

Here’s my previous interview with Shelise if you’d like to check it out:
https://youtu.be/2E6xA02XfZg?si=OnF1vE6KL7MkmVPe

🔔 Be sure to subscribe for more real, unfiltered conversations about life in the JW cult and beyond. I’ve got more powerful interviews with women who’ve left coming soon!

Until next time, keep questioning, keep healing! You’re not alone on this journey. 
With love- Alissa
subjects: The Church Of Jesus Christ Of Latter-Day Saints

episodes.title: I Met With My Jehovah's Witness Dad After 2 Years and It Derailed My Healing Journey
episodes.description:
Hi friends, 
I just wanted to do a quick check-in. Recently, I’ve been struggling mentally after meeting with my dad for the first time in almost two years. Healing after leaving this cult is anything but easy—no matter how much progress you think you’ve made, something can come along and set you back.

My journey will always be honest, so I’m not just going to share the “good” moments. The hard ones matter too! 

That said, I am proud of myself. Even in this setback, I didn’t shrink back into old patterns of people-pleasing like I would have in the past. That shows me my healing is real and still working.

For those also in the healing process, I am with you and here to support in any way I can. 

I will be back with more #exjw Women interviews soon!   And as always I appreciate your support and love. 

-Alissa

episodes.title: My Honest Thoughts on Disassociating from the Jehovah's Witness Religion
episodes.description:
Hello friends!
This is a topic I see argued time and time again in the #exjw world, so I wanted to give my thoughts on it. I compiled a list of all the things we thought about before making our decision. 
I will never push my ideas on anyone, what worked for us doesn't work for everyone, but I am  firm in my belief our path was the correct path for US. 

As always thank you for watching and for your support! 

Please leave in the comments your thoughts on my idea for creating a community for #exjw women/moms. I'd love to hear from you! 

-Alissa

episodes.title: Let's Catch Up | Addressing Criticism | Reacting to New JW Propaganda | WEEKLY VLOG
episodes.description:
Hi friends! It has been almost 2 weeks since I last uploaded! Let's catch up and I will fill you in on what has been going on in life. Also I reacted to a few of the new #jw convention videos that have been released. Let's just say it is getting more unhinged than ever. 

Catch me on an upcoming @CultstoConsciousness podcast! Super excited I got to record with Shelise yesterday. 

And tonight I will be live with Wally @exjehovahswitness so come on over and chat around 6pm MST. 

Comment below what type of video you'd like to see next! Vlog? Day in my life? More deep dives in to #jehovahswitnesses ?  I am always open to suggestions!

And as always, thank you for all the support! I will see you all next time! 
 -Alissa

episodes.title: My JW Parent's Sent My Kids a Letter | Shunning Rant | Easter Sunday | SAHM Weekend Vlog
episodes.description:
Hi friends! This weekend I take you along for my hair appointment and have a little Saturday shunning rant. We received a letter addressed to my kids from my parents who we haven't seen in over a year and a half. It opened a lot of wounds and I had to talk about it. Then, we had our quiet Easter Sunday at home, complete with a backyard Easter egg hunt!

As always, thank you for being here and supporting!
-Alissa

Don't forget to like, subscribe and hit the notification bell for more videos like this! I put out videos every week!

episodes.title: From Fear to Freedom: Life After Leaving the Jehovah’s Witnesses
episodes.description:
Today I just want to talk about what life’s been like after leaving the Jehovah's Witness religion—what’s helped me heal, some of the amazing things I’ve experienced since, and how I’m feeling hopeful about the future.

As always, thank you all for the support you've given me and our little family.

-Alissa
